data.body:
PASS 1 review — G1G2 (edit-set / scope accuracy): CLEAN, no findings.

Verified against src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py and tests:
- All named symbols exist and locations match: _compute_backoff_seconds (L126), _cas_backoff_seconds (L417), constants _BACKOFF_* (L77-81) and _CAS_BACKOFF_* (L100-103), budgets _LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET_DEFAULT=5 / _CAS_RETRY_BUDGET=8, call sites acquire_pass_lock (L260) and _cas_advance_with_retry (L464). No hallucinated edit targets.
- No external consumers to enumerate: both backoff helpers are module-private and referenced ONLY within _advisory_lock.py (each: 1 def + 1 call site). The proposed _jittered_backoff name has zero existing matches (no collision). acquire_pass_lock's outside-dir consumers (__main__.py:414, orchestrate tests) call the public API, not the backoff helpers, so the internal refactor leaves them untouched. The constants are not imported elsewhere (acli_rest.py:50 _BACKOFFS is an unrelated symbol).
- Behavioral classification: pure in-scope refactor (collapse shared math to one parameterized helper + thin constant-binding wrappers; RNG draw count/order, constants, budgets, and call sites all preserved). No CREATION/new behavior. Bounded blast radius is acknowledged and the decision to leave other retry sites bespoke is stated.
